summary: Backend: 21/21 pytest tests passed against all endpoints (auth, tasks CRUD, mood, focus, body-double, shop, AI breakdown/affirmation/empathy, progress, settings, 401 enforcement, XP awarding logic). Frontend: Landing page renders with Get Started CTA, registration flows to onboarding, pricing page shows 3 tiers correctly. Onboarding requires user selection on each of 5 steps before advancing (Next button stays inactive until choice made). Protected routes correctly redirect to /onboarding if user has not completed onboarding flow. No console/page errors detected.

issues: Next button has same yellow active styling whether or not a struggle is selected; visually appears clickable but click is silently ignored. Recommend adding disabled state styling (lower opacity) until selection is made for clearer affordance.

action_items: Optional: Disable/dim Onboarding Next button until at least one option is selected to avoid silent no-op clicks (UX improvement only, not a bug).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 525-line file; consider splitting into routers (auth, tasks, ai, shop) for maintainability as features grow.

award_xp_and_streak: streak grace logic resets grace_days_used to 0 only on consecutive day, but never decays — if user goes diff>1 with grace_used>=2, streak resets correctly but grace persists at 2 only until next reset. Acceptable for MVP.
/api/ai/breakdown raises 500 on parse failure instead of soft fallback; affirmation has fallback. Consider symmetric soft-fallback for breakdown.
Body-double rooms endpoint is unauthenticated — fine for MVP but worth noting for future presence integration.
All endpoints return 200 instead of 201 for creates (tasks/mood/register). Functional but not REST-idiomatic.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (critical flows verified: landing, auth, register→onboarding, pricing; deeper pages gated by onboarding selections, behaving correctly)

test_credentials: Existing test1@focus.com / test123 + dynamic ui_<ts>@focus.com created during testing.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run registers a unique TEST email (test_<uuid>@focus.com) plus tasks/moods/focus sessions/shop purchases for that user. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable (creates fresh user each run). Frontend onboarding requires actually clicking a struggle option before Next advances — Playwright scripts must select an option (e.g., page.click('text=Starting tasks')) before clicking Next on each step.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Onboarding 'no advance on Next' observed during automated testing was correct gating behavior, not a bug — surfaced as a minor UX visibility recommendation.
